paper_title: TG, DTA, and ATR-IR Spectroscopic Analysis of DE and Silica Materials

methods.description: Thermogravimetric (TG) and Differential Thermal Analysis (DTA) are used to study the thermal stability and composition of Nevada-DE, Noto-DE, silica gel, and silica glass by measuring weight loss and endothermic/exothermic reactions as a function of temperature.

steps.description: Collect TG and DTA curves for unheated Nevada-DE, Noto-DE, silica gel, and silica glass.
steps.searchable_summary: collect TG and DTA curves for various DE and silica materials

steps.description: Analyze weight loss patterns and endothermic peaks in TG and DTA curves.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze weight loss and endothermic peaks in TG and DTA data

steps.description: Compare total weight loss and endothermic peaks across different materials.
steps.searchable_summary: compare total weight loss and endothermic peaks across materials

methods.description: Attenuated Total Reflectance Infrared (ATR-IR) spectroscopy is used to analyze the molecular structure and composition of Nevada-DE, Noto-DE, and silica gel by identifying characteristic absorption bands.

steps.description: Collect ATR-IR spectra for unheated and heated Nevada-DE, Noto-DE, and silica gel.
steps.searchable_summary: collect ATR-IR spectra for DE and silica materials

steps.description: Identify and analyze characteristic absorption bands in ATR-IR spectra.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and analyze absorption bands in ATR-IR spectra

steps.description: Compare ATR-IR spectra across different materials and temperatures.
steps.searchable_summary: compare ATR-IR spectra across materials and temperatures
